mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 6 months signals rising inflation expectations, leading to a decrease in the purchasing power of consumers and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over 3 months signals a tightening labor market, leading to increased wage pressures and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in the number of Housing Starts (HOUST) over 6 months signals a strengthening housing market, leading to increased consumer spending and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) over 3 months signals a tightening monetary cycle, leading to decreased borrowing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% decrease in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over 6 months signals a weakening supply chain, leading to decreased productivity and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3% increase in the Real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) over 6 months signals a strengthening economy, leading to increased consumer spending and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only when the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) is below 5%.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises > 3% in 6 months and UNRATE < 5%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the number of Payroll Employment (PAYEMS) over 6 months signals a strengthening labor market, leading to increased wage pressures and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, but only when the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is above 2%.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 2% in 6 months and FEDFUNDS > 2%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
